---
title: 'My God! The impact of owning your relationship with God.'
date: '2021-02-06T22:37:00.0010000+00:00'
draft: true
blogger_blog: 'Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller'
blogger_status: 'LIVE'
blogger_post_id: '3294785167005359305'
blogger_filename: '/2021/02/my-god-impact-of-owning-your.html'
blogger_source: 'takeout-20260223T235336Z-3-001.zip:Takeout/Blogger/Blogs/Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller/feed.atom'
---

<div>Jacob (renamed Israel) had multiple experiences, including breakthrough experiences, with the God of Abraham and Isaac. Now he was ready to declare El Elohe Israel.</div><div><br></div><div>The God, is now my God...</div><div><br></div><div>I heard about this God, El, having met with my father, and grandfather before him... But now I have had enough experience with Him to call Him My God.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>Jacob built a permanent structure dedicated to El, aka El Elyon, aka El Shaddai, aka YHWH.</div><div><br></div><div>That dedication would have lasting ramifications.</div><div><br></div><div>In a few short years, Jacob's son Joseph (at 13) would go to Egypt and prepare a safety net for the whole family. One day, nearly 400+ years later, Joseph's descendants would take back Canaan and reburry his bones in the city of this marker.</div><div><br></div><div>Go ahead, have experiences of your own, but if you will reach a place where <i>the</i> God of heaven becomes <i>your</i> God, it will have lasting impacts.</div><div><br></div><div>Selah</div><div><br></div><div><br></div><div>Genesis 33:17â€“20 (LEB): 17&nbsp;But Jacob traveled on to Succoth, and he built for himself a house, and he made shelters for his livestock. Therefore he called the name of the place Succoth. 18&nbsp;And Jacob came safely to the city of Shechem which is in the land of Canaan, âŒŠon his wayâŒ‹ from Paddan-Aram. And he camped before the city. 19&nbsp;And he bought a piece of land where he pitched his tent for one hundred pieces of money from the hand of the sons of Hamor, father of Shechem. 20&nbsp;And there he erected an altar and called it â€œEl Elohe Israel.â€&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div><br></div><div><div class="separator" style="clear: both; text-align: center;">
  <a href="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/1612651038876890-0-a9664f6aa558.png" imageanchor="1" style="margin-left: 1em; margin-right: 1em;">
    <img border="0"   src="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/1612651038876890-0-a9664f6aa558.png" width="400">
  </a>
</div><div class="separator" style="clear: both; text-align: center;">
  <a href="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/1612651035709868-1-39a73a72ff5b.png" imageanchor="1" style="margin-left: 1em; margin-right: 1em;">
    <img border="0"   src="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/1612651035709868-1-39a73a72ff5b.png" width="400">
  </a>
</div><div class="separator" style="clear: both; text-align: center;">
  <a href="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/1612651032176283-2-c62f3feb3cc8.png" imageanchor="1" style="margin-left: 1em; margin-right: 1em;">
    <img border="0"   src="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/1612651032176283-2-c62f3feb3cc8.png" width="400">
  </a>
</div><br></div>
